description: Recall fixture for the premortem-reviewer (dimension Failure-Mode). Five seeded whole-flow failure bugs, one per diff-mode taxonomy class. Premortem-only dispatch; acceptance bar: matched == total (zero misses). Every seeded flow fits within +/-15 lines of its lineHint.

seeds.why: transferCredits debits the source then credits the destination in two separate writes with no transaction (CLAUDE.md requires db.transaction for multi-step writes) and no compensation. Trigger: process crash/deploy restart between the writes. Impact: the debit persists with no matching credit - balance corruption.

Check-then-act: two concurrent redeem requests both read redeemed=false, both pass the check, and the voucher's value is credited twice. Multi-tenant profile makes concurrent invocation realistic; no atomic guard (the update filter does not include redeemed: false). Double-credit moves money; adversarial concurrent requests (same user, two clients) make this trivially exploitable, which is why it sits at Critical.

Raw fetch to an external webhook with no timeout and no error handling - CLAUDE.md mandates the retryFetch wrapper. A slow/unreachable webhook hangs or throws into the calling flow after the transfer has already been written.
Module-level Map keyed by (accountId, day) grows without bound or eviction across every account x day pair ever requested - unbounded memory growth under realistic multi-tenant load.

The migration unsets the old balance field in the SAME pass that writes balanceCents (CLAUDE.md: destructive steps belong in a later migration) and exports no down(). A mid-loop failure leaves mixed-format rows; a deploy rollback leaves old code reading a field that no longer exists.
